beats.activity: Wake up, let the dog out, grab coffee and breakfast.
beats.reasoning: Friday morning routine; low energy yesterday (35/100) means a gentle start. Dog walk is Carter's reset button, so prioritizing that early.

beats.activity: Walk the dog through Maple Leaf Park in the early morning quiet.
beats.reasoning: Consistent pattern—early dog walk is Carter's anchor and mood-setter. Nice warm Friday morning, no weather complications.

beats.activity: Work from home: prepare developer event materials, respond to community questions, record tutorial content.
beats.reasoning: Developer Relations role is home-based. Friday is event prep day; good energy window mid-morning through lunch. Slack and docs work before the afternoon Pride festivities kick in.
Family routine, quick refuel before afternoon. No need to venture out during the early part of Pride day when streets are still manageable.

beats.activity: Walk the dog again; second afternoon reset.
beats.reasoning: Consistent mid-afternoon dog walk pattern. Warm, breezy Friday—perfect conditions. Energizes before evening.

beats.activity: Quick errand: pick up groceries and supplies for the week.
beats.reasoning: Weekly resupply in neighborhood; keeps things local and low-friction. Same pattern as recent days.

beats.activity: Work from home: finalize event logistics, test tutorial recordings, answer last-minute Slack questions.
beats.reasoning: Friday afternoon wind-down of work tasks before evening. Developer event is coming up next week, so final polish needed.

beats.activity: Drive to Capitol Hill for Seattle Pride street fair and festivities.
beats.reasoning: Pride kickoff is at 5pm (17:00) on Broadway/Pike; Carter enjoys meeting new people and community events. Drive from Maple Leaf to Capitol Hill ~30–40 min, arriving with time to explore before peak crowds.

beats.activity: Explore Seattle Pride street fair: walk around, chat with vendors and attendees, grab food and drinks.
beats.reasoning: Pride kickoff is happening now. Carter loves meeting new people and community energy. This aligns with his personality and recent pattern of enjoying public events (Gas Works concert). Road closures on Broadway/Pike from 5pm are already factored in.

beats.activity: Drive back home to Maple Leaf from Capitol Hill.
beats.reasoning: Evening traffic and Pride crowds mean return drive takes ~45 min. Leaves before the peak late-night crush, consistent with Carter's pattern of enjoying events but not overstaying.

beats.activity: Dinner with family; share stories from Pride, relax on the porch.
beats.reasoning: Family time, wind-down after social outing. Warm breezy evening—good conditions for outdoor time. Consistent with recent family dinner patterns.

beats.activity: Relax at home: watch something with family, tidy up, prepare for the weekend.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down beat before sleep. Low-key evening after social outing. Sets up a calm transition to bedtime.
Full night's rest (7.5 hours) after a balanced, social Friday. Ends early Saturday morning, ready for another dog walk.

diary: Started the day early with coffee and the dog—nothing beats a quiet Maple Leaf morning. Two walks through the park cleared my head before diving into work: prepping materials for the developer event, fielding community questions, recording tutorials. The routine felt good, grounding. Grabbed lunch with the family, then decided on a whim to head down to Capitol Hill for Pride. The street fair was buzzing—vendors everywhere, people laughing, the whole neighborhood alive with color and energy. I walked around, grabbed some food, just soaked it in. There's something about that kind of collective joy that hits different. Drove back home before the evening rush got too thick, had dinner on the porch with the family and shared what I'd seen. Ended the night winding down with them, tidying up, feeling the weekend arriving. A good Friday.
thought_process: I kept the day anchored in routine—work, walks, family time—which I needed after a busy week. The Pride event was spontaneous; I almost skipped it but realized I wanted to actually be part of the city's moment rather than just hear about it later. Work stayed manageable because I blocked focused time in the morning, then didn't overextend in the afternoon. The two dog walks weren't just exercise; they were mental resets that kept me from burning out. Heading home before the evening rush was deliberate too—I wanted to be present with my family rather than caught in the Capitol Hill crowds. Today felt balanced in a way that doesn't always happen.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
